description: LEFT JOIN makes all right-side columns nullable, including those already nullable in schema

columns.name: total
columns.note: NOT NULL in schema but nullable due to LEFT JOIN

columns.name: notes
columns.note: already nullable in schema, still nullable

columns.name: created_at
columns.note: NOT NULL in schema but nullable due to LEFT JOIN

name: right_side_multiple_cols
query_sql:
-- @name GetUserWithOrderDetails
-- @returns :many
SELECT u.name, o.total, o.notes, o.created_at FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.id = o.user_id
schema_sql: CREATE TABLE users (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, name TEXT NOT NULL, email TEXT NOT NULL, bio TEXT, age INTEGER, score NUMERIC, status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'active', created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())
CREATE TABLE orders (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, user_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id), total INTEGER NOT NULL, discount INTEGER, notes TEXT, created_at TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now())

sqlc_comparison.scythe_improvement: scythe correctly marks all right-side columns as nullable due to LEFT JOIN semantics
sqlc_comparison.sqlc_behavior: sqlc marks o.total and o.created_at as non-nullable based on schema NOT NULL constraints
